name: Caelin Sutch
why_now: In May 2026, Caelin co-published the National Design Studio's public account of making AI useful in high-friction government services, after moving onto its technical staff.
signal_summary: Co-authored National Design Studio's May 2026 public note on applying AI to hard-to-use government systems.

name: Simo Ryu
why_now: Simo's public GitHub showed fresh July 2026 activity alongside a sustained body of minimal, widely adopted diffusion-model implementations.

name: Louis Castricato
why_now: Overworld published Waypoint 1.5 in June 2026, putting Louis on a small team shipping a real-time interactive video world model for consumer hardware.
signal_summary: Named on the Waypoint 1.5 team shipping an interactive video world model for consumer hardware.

name: Katherine "Kati" Michel
why_now: Kati's public GitHub showed continued output in July 2026 across 80-plus repositories, building on years of hands-on open-source maintainership.

name: Mete Ismayilzada
why_now: Mete began a Microsoft Research internship in April 2026 on LLMs for scientific discovery while continuing his EPFL computational-creativity PhD.

name: Ashverya Sheth
why_now: Ashverya moved into a Zoox technical program internship in May 2026 while pursuing Wharton's AI-for-business MBA track.

name: Siddharth Uppal
why_now: Siddharth began investing at BMW i Ventures in January 2026, working across AI, enterprise software, mobility, robotics, and industrial technology.

name: Sawit Koseeyaumporn
why_now: Sawit won the 2025 BDI AI and data hackathon with a five-person student team and is now publicly shipping machine-learning projects from Bangkok.
school: King Mongkut's University of Technology Thonburi
signal_summary: Named by public competition coverage as a member of the student team that won the 2025 BDI AI and Data Innovation hackathon.

name: Jeroen Op de Beek
why_now: Jeroen's TU Delft team won the February 2026 ICPC Europe Championship, defending its title and qualifying for the November 2026 World Finals.
signal_summary: TU Delft named Jeroen on its team that won the 2026 ICPC Europe Championship and qualified for the World Finals.

name: Toni Brajko
why_now: Toni's University of Zagreb team placed seventh among 140 teams at the September 2025 ICPC World Finals, earning silver and the European title.
signal_summary: The Croatian Informatics Association named Toni on Zagreb's seventh-place, silver-medal ICPC World Finals team.
